page: The Vultr vx1-m-64c-512gb is a memory-optimized virtual server instance belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC-Turin processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The configuration delivers 64 vCPUs and 64 physical cores running at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz, with a single thread per core. It is equipped with 512.0 GB of system memory, providing a high resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Cache specifications include 48 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache.

In synthetic benchmarks, the instance demonstrates strong compute capabilities despite its shared CPU allocation. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance benchmark registers at 3710.38, placing it in the top tier (top 10%). Multi-core performance under stress-ng reaches 118152.34, which is categorized as strong (top 25%). The processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 vector extensions (AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, AVX512_BF16) and hardware-accelerated cryptography (AES, VAES, SHA_NI), which accelerate vector math and secure operations.

Storage is limited to a 1 GB network-attached local disk, meaning primary data storage must be handled via external network volumes. The instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. A key architectural tradeoff is the shared CPU allocation, which may introduce resource contention under sustained multi-core workloads. However, this shared model provides a highly cost-effective path for memory-intensive applications that require massive RAM capacity without needing dedicated, continuous CPU execution.

This server configuration is suited for memory-intensive workloads that do not require sustained, non-shared CPU performance. Appropriate use cases include large-scale caching layers, in-memory databases, data buffering, analytical workloads with moderate CPU demands, and development or staging environments for memory-heavy enterprise applications.
description: The Vultr vx1-m-64c-512gb is an x86_64 virtual server from the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring 64 vCPUs and 64 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC-Turin processor. It is configured with 512.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high density of 8.0 GB of RAM per core, alongside a minimal 1 GB network-attached boot disk. Although it utilizes a shared CPU allocation, benchmarks show strong performance, with stress-ng single-core results in the top 10% and multi-core in the top 25%. The processor supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and VAES. This instance offers a cost-effective profile for memory-heavy workloads, trading dedicated CPU execution for massive memory capacity. It is well-suited for large in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive analytical tasks with moderate CPU utilization.
og_description: Vultr vx1-m-64c-512gb is a VX1 Cloud Compute server with 64 AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and shared CPU allocation, delivering top-tier single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-m-64c-512gb is a memory-optimized server with 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and shared CPU allocation for memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized virtual server featuring sixty-four AMD EPYC cores and half a terabyte of RAM for memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 64 AMD EPYC-Turin physical cores with x86_64 architecture
512.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core
Shared CPU allocation offering a cost-effective memory-dense profile
Top-tier single-core and strong multi-core stress-ng benchmark performance
1 GB network-attached local storage and one public IPv4 address
Suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-heavy analytics
